original: [flags.SOPHORA_MET |[var2 |What have you gotten yourself into... [pc.isHPDefeated |dragging this broken, near-lifeless body around so dangerous a place... |forgetting to sate your needs in Zhèng Shi of all places... ] From the shadows, she comes; fears in the far back of your mind come to life as the long muscled arms of your amazonian pursuer close around your neck, lifting you from the ground; vile, guttural sounds hiss into your [pc.ear] as your reality is strangled. Zhèng Shi swims into a nauseous ocean. In the next few seconds, you know very well what it’s like to be a corpse. And also what it’s like to return to your body, unable to even gasp. If not for the deafening snap of handcuffs to your wrists, you’d think you’d lost all motor control. The weight of a waking nightmare leans into your back, knee pressed into a particular spot of your spine. “Hrmff......” |Bounty hunters, mercenaries... they’re all bought-and-paid-for tools used to settle accounts. Knowing that you’ve made the highest powers on ZS angry is the first step in defending yourself from the force of retaliation you know only as Sophora. Now that the ink-black Jumper has spent her element of surprise, you can detect her — you’ve learned to detect the fluctuations in your surroundings when her baleful eyes linger on you. [rand |You treat every corner like a booby trap. Whosoever might mock you as paranoid wouldn’t have dodged that punch just now. They’d be eating their own teeth. |Every shadow is an enemy. At any moment, the black rabbit killer could emerge from a puddle — and she does, with a leaping slash of her sword that you manage to avoid. |Like a mindless automaton she rounds the next corner and marches in your direction, unflinching brutal gaze locked to you like an aspect seeker. |Having to look over your shoulder every ten seconds makes journeying through the station’s underbelly a harrowing ordeal; yet... it’s why you manage to spot your stalker before she gets too close, forcing your silent threat into the light. ] White-blue energy forms into a cryonic saber as you stare down the dot-sight of a high-powered pistol. “Hhhfff...” the laquine hisses gutturally, as if to say, you’re trapped in here with me, now. Sophora, the one-bun vendetta, has come for you again. ] |[var0 |These black, gloomy mines are a miserable warren to navigate. The living aren’t meant to wander the deep, deep dark for long.[silly | You could be snatched from existence by a grue, for example. ] But you detect an innate hatred exuding from the dank darkness, as though a malign being were tracking your motions. Jumpers[var1 |, snakes, and robots | and robots ] are threats that you know. Rubber squeaks and metal stomps are easy to avoid. This disgusting dread swells, a chewing tinfoil feeling in your gut; a night terror come alive stalks the labyrinth. [pc.reflexesRange 66 |Never before have your survival instincts burned so hot. It happens so fast: a dangerous gleam around the next corner; a fist that almost connects with your jaw; a long arm; shock and ardor pump into your heart as you square off against the approaching shadow. Sweat beads as your assailant approaches, grim and arch — calm malevolence sealed beneath an ocean of shifting tar. |An explosion of sinew detonates in your head as the mother of all sucker punches emerges from the void and sends you reeling. Shock and adrenaline are pumped into your heart as you sprawl across jagged stone. Raising your [pc.weapon] to keep the shadow at bay, you stagger away into a shaky combat stance, straining your [pc.eyes] to the grim arch of your approaching assailant — malevolent calm sealed beneath an ocean of shifting tar. ] |Zhèng Shi’s foundry is a busy, well-traveled zone monitored by a twisted, ever-vigilant group of cybernetically-enhanced kaithrit, though even in the midst of all this activity, there’s a chewing of tinfoil inside your gut. Something malicious — that feeling from before — creeps up on you, lithe, chilling tendrils crawling up the back of your neck. A sudden cold air touches your [pc.skinFurScalesNoun] under the [pc.ear], like the breath of a ghost. [pc.reflexesRange 66 |Traversing the Rush has been a baptism by fire for your survival instincts. You leap like a startled tiger, scrunching up into a tumble and roll that has you staring down the outstretched punch of your assailant, [pc.weapon] raised. Unfazed, the evil spirit approaches, clad in armor so black that it seems like shifting tar. Disturbing, calm malevolence. |Brushing it off was a mistake. A strong hand clamps to your shoulder and whirls you around. Everything after that is a mush of bruised colors. The sucker punch to top all sneak attacks sends you reeling across the floor; shock and adrenaline pump into your heart. Even with your [pc.weapon] in hand, you feel a special kind of panic coming on, like the universe has compacted in an attempt to swallow you. Making out your approaching assailant, a grim, arch figure of malevolent calm, isn’t easy. ] |To float through the upper levels of the ZS foundry is to pass by the workshops and material dumps that supply the pirate lords with their vast means to terrorize the frontier. Security is tight, activity is heavy... and your gut instinct clamors in your mind like a hundred voices all at once. You stop breathing. Something dreadful approaches. Did you forget it, were you not paying attention? Just what is this cold ire you find yourself transfixed by? [pc.reflexesRange 66 |Staying focused is why you catch the dangerous glint in the shadows. Out of pure nothingness, a sucker punch launches that you can feel move the air as you throw yourself back. You wipe the glaze from your eyes and sweep the proximity with your [pc.weapon], meeting the grim, arch eyes of your approaching assailant — calm malevolence armored in an ocean of shifting tar. |Unfortunately, the question is not answered on your terms. From out of pure nothingness a jaw-cracking sucker punch sends you reeling across the scaffolding in shock and agony. Everything aches so badly that you can feel every swishing, needling throb. Your spine crawls as you gaze up, [pc.weapon] in hand, to face your approaching assailant: looming silence, grim and arch — malevolent calm clad in an ocean of shifting tar. ] ] ]

original: Not an illusion, a tall, looming creature stalks your way. Cold and unreadable — a professional killer. The humanoid figure seethes with a presence uncommon in the rank and file pirate. You work your mind feverishly to understand your opponent: inky, oily hair sits on its shoulders; that tight, soundless suit goes all the way up to its — Her chin. Short muzzle, shoulder-length ears and hair, fur — everything is black: it’s a laquine, maybe a Jumper. Unlike any of her airheaded confederates, this one’s lapine features hold the depth of unimaginable cruelty. Looped around her waist is their signature shield belt equipped with jet-boosters and holsters for two weapons; she’s loaded for bear, ammo, weapons, boosters, a tac harness... with each noiseless step she takes, you eke out wide female curves and distinct male bulges; a sleek vambrace hugs her left forearm. “Kkhh...” In the blink of an eye, she arms herself with a gun in one hand and a cryonic sword in the other, its turquoise-blue energy blade shedding deathly frost — it’s not an attachment of her arm, but the way it blends in, it might as well be a [silly |soul reaver |wraithblade of some sort ] — and there you stand at the points of both. You have to defend yourself.

original: [var0 |Mhen’ga... it all started here, under the canopies, beneath the boughs. Despite its familiarity, you nevertheless wander its whispering wilds with caution. The further you traverse the frontier and this Rush, the worse things will get. Seeing beautiful alien nature at work, wondering how many bright-eyed researchers came through to observe strangely butterfly-like insects and dragonfly-shaped bugs carrying out their work... it’s calming. Something falls. The forest goes silent. Your [pc.ears] perk; you raise your [pc.weapon]; the underbrush rustles and crackles. An oppressive sense of dread descends. “Vrrgghh...” [var1 |...It’s no use. Seeing that rabbit again brings to mind [pc.isHPDefeated |the severe, untreated wounds you’ve tried to ignore |your ineradicable desires ]. In the hopes of avoiding her wrath, you collapse unceremoniously... almost playing dead. Unfortunately, she’s not a primitive bear. Subdual follows, your [pc.arms] twisted behind your back into a stress position, blood-chilling energy-cuffs snapped to your wrists. No mercy for the weak; no quarter given for surrender. Whatever she wants you for, even to kill you, she could do it. Beneath the obscuring boughs... will it all end here...? |Thickening sweat trickles down your cheek — it’s Sophora! The laquine maneuvers between the trees, the foliage; she lunges; your eyes blur watching her shift. This is not a dream — how did she find you here...!? ] |A less stalwart Rusher would lose their nerve and send out SOS after wandering the untamed wastes of Tarkus for half a day. Consider its wheezing horizons, the endless nothing existing between massive, jagged spires of scrap and junk. Despite conquering this planet on your own journey, certain thoughts claw at you... certain worries. The further you launch into the frontier, the more enemies you find, the greater the dangers you come across. Some of them follow you. It’s not paranoia. Something has its eye on you, ever since Sophora... you can’t shake the anxiety. You lose your calm state of mind. Your [pc.hands] shake. A paranoid writhing crawls up your [pc.arms] like a swarm of woodlice. Why is there - [var1 |A cinematic entrance throws you on your ass. Metal and debris fly everywhere as an all-black figure blows through the wreckage, landing cleanly on your body. Sophora...! A fist winds behind the killer bun’s eyes[silly |; in short, she punches you right in the soul ], and there’s nothing your [pc.isHPDefeated |severely-wounded self could have done about it |debilitated self could have done to prevent it ]. When you awaken, breaths coming ragged, your bloodstream is frozen by the cuffs clinked about your wrists. Some limp struggling alerts your mind to the weight of your attacker kneeing into your lower vertebrae. It’s really over. |Sophora’s booming entrance — leaping through a mountain of debris and scrap — would be impressive if she weren’t a crazed predator. Sweat rolls down your [pc.face] as the wretched laquine lands crouched, cranes her head to methodically inspect her prey, then stands. Grasping for your [pc.weapon], you take one step back as she breaks into a charge. ] |Myrellion is at a stalemate, but that doesn’t make the dark corners of its cavernous bowels any less spooky. Everywhere’s a tight, filthy fit; some ends gape, others give way to deathpits and traps. Nothing could find you here. If a nuclear holocaust happened, you’d be safe in the crust of a planet not your own. Right? Down here, with all the creepy crawlies, the nyrea... and whatever else lurks in forgotten places... [var1 |It’s no place to be [pc.isHPDefeated |crippled and suffering as you are |horny and ready to kneel for the first fuck in sight regardless of their ulterior motives ]. You know that... and maybe you can get out if you focus. Not a chance: the darkness itself manifests as two thick tendrils closing around your head, pulling you back from whence you came. Assault and unconsciousness happen so fast that you don’t even realize you were caught and subdued before waking up in chilling handcuffs, a strong knee digging into a pressure spot on your spine. A tongue licks up the side of your [pc.ear]. “Hrmmm...” Sophora has caught you. |In the bleak and nothing, when your other senses heighten to cover for the deficiencies in their siblings, a single off-key footstep is a deafening gunshot that shakes your [pc.ears]. Like some super cool holo-star, you wheel 180 with your [pc.weapon] drawn, gaze blearily into the dark eyes of a creeping fever. “Hffffmmmm...” Ice-blue energy forms into a familiar sword that illuminates the ominous laquine’s presence. “Hmphh!” Sophora has tracked you. ] |The Gyre’s density swallows you up. It feels good to be nobody like this, even if you are an off-worlder and stick out a bit from cultural norms. Feels good to be alone. Don’t attract attention and you can move unseen. It’s better that way. Some caustic zaika can’t help but spit their venom, taunt you, mock you, but all assaults on these particular streets remain verbal. Hope it keeps that way. Besides, they were probably talking to the other Rushers nearby, sneering imperialists arrogantly remarking on their barking harassers’ squalid conditions. [var1 |None of them have noticed the way you carry yourself, [pc.isHPDefeated |wounded beyond all reason — you shouldn’t even be standing |horny enough to start a sprawling orgy ]. Hopefully this cover holds out. A figure of all black leaps out from the nearest alleyway. You don’t even have time to realize you’ve been snatched away into the dark, unseen. You don’t make a peep, a huge gloved hand planted firmly over your mouth and nose, and the other gripped tight to your neck. All you manage in the sensory chaos is a look down into bright pink sadist’s eyes, crackling with hatred. It all goes black. You are nothing. Detached from the realm of the living, and unceremoniously returned... face down, [pc.arms] behind your back, hands locked together — freezing. A firm knee jabs into your back, and now you remember: Sophora. A perfect, traceless ambush. She’s found you, even here on Dhaal... “Hmmhmhm...” |You can actually hear the ambience tick down to timeless pause when a shadowy humanoid emerges from the nearby alleyway into your field of view. Smoldering eyes laser-focus on yours, and a pair of hands reach out. Muscles and reflexes you barely knew exist kick into action; they’re not fast enough to avoid the grapple, but they’re enough to free yourself from it... and send you further away from the public into a private battleground. “Kfff...!” Sophora hisses, drawing gun and blade. Nobody can help you here. Your pursuer leaps into action. ] ]

original: A troupe of kaithrit play music while one dressed in long, flowing slips of orange and gold dances for a crowd. She’s a mesmerizing, engaging performer who purrs in the ears of generous benefactors: many credit chits are deposited into a prominent donation box. You watch for a while, which is the point, seeing how she flashes cleavage and adroitly evades the sneaky hands looking to cop a feel. But when her body moves, revealing the men and women opposite, your pupils shrink. It’s a fight-or-flight response. You blink, rub them, and what was there is gone. It was a figure in all black, you swear. In darkness was she walking. What should be a quaint attraction feels like a prison. Muscling your way out of the entrapment, you scan your environment. Human in a black suit... black-and-green styled punky ausar... gray and black leithan... Black laquine. No way. Again you blink. It can’t be... “Watch it!” Your sight is broken by the impolite shove of one of the universe’s innumerable dickheads, carrying himself like the promenade’s stuck-up owner. Sophora -- no, you can’t know that. The laquine you saw is gone. Maybe it’s just your mind playing tricks on you. There’s another black-furred laquine, whose ears reach for the sky. A happy woman, by your quick estimate. It doesn’t do anything for your heart.

original: “Hey hello, how’re you doing?” A down-to-earth-looking guy with a glowing bronze complexion waves you into his group. Apparently, they need a third opinion on who’s telling the best joke today. Sure, you can humor them. Each one’s quite a character, gleaming with the sweat of a hard day’s job. Pretty soon you’re laughing and smiling with them as though you’d known them for your whole life. One playfully elbows you in the ribs. “So, which one do you like?” Before you can even open your mouth, you catch something over his shoulder. The mirth visibly fades from your [pc.face], something they pick up on. Everyone looks down the alley. Nothing. But... you saw something... something lapine-shaped. Something with red eyes. Maybe it’s just the heat. All’s quiet. Another claps your shoulder. “You alright? Wanna get a drink? It’s fuckin’ killer out today.” If you weren’t suddenly paranoid, you might have agreed. Polite farewells are exchanged, and here you’re standing alone among milling colonists. Your [pc.eyes] are on the shadows between buildings, unblinkingly searching. Sophora can’t be here. No way. And there’s no way she can attack you in town. You exhale, coolness injecting into your bloodstream. Maybe your microsurgeons have anti-anxiety functions. It’s welcome. Everything is going to be alright.
context: <> textify(['\\n\\t\\t“Hey hello, how’re you doing?”\\n\\n\\t\\'...])

original:
Getting bored with Novahome is the remotest possibility. The native raskvel inhabiting it are exploding with energy twenty-four-seven, hawking items of dubious value, entertaining their guests, lending long, long ears to tall tales beyond Tarkus’s atmosphere. Smiling at the big picture comes easy: there’s always some new, convivial detail to glance at; the fervor in this shabby comfort never wanes.

		Not for them, but for you. Your mind’s eye detects that which it associates the purest kind of hair-raising anxiety with. The corridors around you mute as your pupils shrink, fight or flight response stirring. You stand idle there and for a moment strain to detect... what that was. Something in all black. Something with disturbing eyes.

		...Sophora? No... no, it was just a raskvel’s ears. Look at that pair in the distance, one raskvel perched on the shoulders of another, darker scales than usual. Shake your head. Nothing.

		You turn, decide to get scarce, and there it is again. Is it? Something rabbit-like staring at you and drifting from sight.

		Suddenly, you notice none of the excitement. Everything feels drab. Isolated. Nothing would dare strike at you here, still... still. That Jumper’s living in your head rent-free...

original: “Oh, he did more than that,” you answer with a wink and a hair-ruffling gust of wind. “Dad gave me the opportunity to dip my toes into humanity’s best psionic enhancement programs, and it turns out at least one of my parents had more than a little aptitude for telekinesis. Sure, I had to undergo all kinds of treatments and augmentations to access my powers, but it was worth it - mostly. The year I got my final psi amp installed, I had to spend months as the bald kid because Reinkurt Station couldn’t be bothered to have a decent cosmetics facility to regrow my hair.” You hide the residual emotional damage beneath a wry chuckle. “Teasing the kid that’s learning to throw invisible punches with [pc.hisHer] brain wasn’t a very smart idea.” Bianca gasps, “Children can be so cruel.” You nod, then chuckle, “Yeah I was basically a young adult at that point, and plenty of kids are out there catching flak for things a lot less cool than becoming some kind of space wizard. Any child that makes it through their youth without picking up a few issues is some kind of unicorn, or universal lottery winner. I did a lot better than most, and look!” You carefully float your Codex through the air between you, making in dance in beautiful geometrical patterns that defy the imagination. “I don’t need a weapon. I don’t need tools. I don’t need equipment. I don’t need to hit the gym. I just need to focus, and I can do almost anything.” "There’s limits right? You said something about telekinesis, but I hear there’s other kinds of mind powers out there too, like telepathy.” "Yeah I have dick-all affinity for telepathy. The most I can do is whisper a short sentence into someone’s head, and I might not even manage that. I’m just not wired for that kind of gift. Heck, I couldn’t even read a cat’s mind, let alone a kaithrit’s.” You shake your head sadly. Telepathy would be so useful on the Rush! Bianca props her face up prettily. “Don’t the terrans have a saying about that?” “Fuck Psykers?” She playfully slaps your shoulder. “No, silly! The grass is always greener on the other side.” “Oh yeah, that one’s pretty great too.” The conversation rolls on for hours as she picks your brain about your abilities, interspersing bits of folksy wisdom here among philosophical discussions about the nature of extra-natural powers that fly in the face of most models used in physics. Somehow the dialogue left you feeling even better about your decision to become a kineticist.

original: You must have said something wrong as the kaithrit woman tosses her head back and unleashes a frustrated groan. “EVERYONE ON THIS DAMNED STATION IS A BOTTOM!” Deenya screams, and you feel eyes from all around the bar turn to your table. The cat woman returns each look with venom and stands. “That’s right! Bottoms, all of you!” She points an accusatory finger at the patrons and continues, “Everyone here bends over backwards for a piece of dick! A little whiff and you all turn servile and pathetic!” You shoot up from your chair and put your arms on Deenya’s shoulders, trying to calm the woman down before she gets her shit kicked in by every boot on the station. “Deenya, Deenya!” You try to speak over her rant, “DEENYA!” You scream and clamp down onto her shoulders, pushing her back down into her chair. “Sorry, everyone,” you say to the room, “She got a little excited.” Slowly, everyone’s attention returns to their own business and the gentle murmur once again fills the air. With a sigh of relief you slump back into your chair. You give your date a frustrated look that turns to confusion as you see her with her mouth and eyes agape and ears twitching. She quickly collects herself and smiles. “Sorry about that,” she starts and continues in a hushed tone, “But it’s true! Every single time I take someone out for a romp, they grovel and beg for me to fuck them ’like the sluts they are’! I don’t want that!” she says almost pleadingly. “I want someone strong who will fight me! Who won’t just lie down and let me do what I want! Can you be that someone? Please tell me those muscles aren’t just for show!” she reaches out to squeeze your bicep for emphasis. You lean back into your chair and mull it over in your head. She wants a partner who will challenge her for domination during sex, yet is confident enough that her expectations for the night didn’t even humor the chance of you succeeding. You can safely assume that all she cares about is being on top and a show - a token one at the very least - of defiance from her partner. One thing is for sure: she is bound to work up a sweat and make it your problem.
